page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processors using the Neoverse-N1 model. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate solid multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the average tier. Single-core performance represents a relative tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the lower tier. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 1894.03 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 173.34 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The local NVMe SSD storage supports high-speed local data access, making it efficient for workloads requiring low-latency scratch space.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and lower power consumption compared to traditional x86 architectures, translating to a favorable performance-per-resource ratio for multi-threaded applications. This instance is appropriate for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from local SSD storage, such as database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, though single-core processing represents a relative tradeoff. The custom ARM architecture offers qualitative cost efficiency through high resource density and lower power consumption. This instance is appropriate for multi-threaded workloads requiring high-speed local storage, such as database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-performance workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 cores at 2.5 GHz
Includes 1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Appropriate for databases, software compilation, and static web serving
